steps.title: Find the slope of line γ
steps.content: When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals. Since line $k$ has slope $m$, line $\gamma$ must have slope $-\frac{1}{m}$.

steps.title: Use the slope to find the point on γ when x equals 8
steps.content: Line $\gamma$ passes through $(7, 5)$ with slope $-\frac{1}{m}$. When we move from $x = 7$ to $x = 8$ (a horizontal change of 1), the vertical change is: $$\Delta y = -\frac{1}{m} \cdot 1 = -\frac{1}{m}$$ So the $y$-coordinate at $x = 8$ is $5 - \frac{1}{m}$, making the point $(8, 5 - \frac{1}{m})$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
